title.rendered: Porta Cabin in Delhi: SAMAN’s Factory-Direct Guide to Sizes, Prices & Greater Noida Delivery

content.rendered:
Buyers searching for a porta cabin in Delhi in 2026 see one anchor everywhere on the SERP — ₹1,170 to ₹1,430 per square foot — and almost nothing else. What that headline doesn’t tell you: what a 10×20 unit actually costs delivered, how a Delhi NCT 10 PM heavy-vehicle entry rule reshapes your lead time, or whether the cabin’s warranty covers what fails first. This is what we publish for Delhi buyers — real numbers, real spec floors, and the routing logic from our Greater Noida unit at Jalpura. SAMAN ships porta cabins to Delhi addresses across south, west, north, and central zones in 1 to 7 days depending on size and spec.

The Four Buyer Zones in Delhi: Where Porta Cabins Actually Get Used

A porta cabin in Delhi typically lands in one of four buyer contexts, and the right size depends more on the context than the catalogue. South Delhi — Greater Kailash, Hauz Khas, Saket, Vasant Kunj — orders 6×6 security cabins for housing-society guard posts and 10×10 sales offices for residential plot listings. Security cabins for housing societies in south Delhi most often use our 6×6 ft mini porta cabin format, set on a 4-bolt base on the boundary-wall side. West Delhi — Mayapuri, Naraina, Kirti Nagar — runs SME factory campuses and wholesale yards; the common order is a 10×20 site office with toilet for shop-floor supervisors, often paired with a 6×6 security cabin at the gate. North Delhi — Bawana, Narela, Sahibabad border — sees the larger volumes: 20×10 team offices for construction contractors, 30×10 engineering rooms for project HQs, multi-room layouts for labour quarters next to active sites. Central Delhi — Daryaganj, ITO, government complexes — books shorter-cycle modular porta cabin rentals: portable structures for institutional renovations, election-related polling-team accommodation, event-site sales kiosks. Central-Delhi cabin needs are typically 3-12 month cycles tied to specific projects, so rental usually wins over purchase.

What’s the Per-Size Cost of a Porta Cabin in Delhi? (Real Numbers, Not Just ₹/Sq Ft)

A porta cabin in Delhi costs ₹1,170 to ₹1,430 per square foot in 2026 — that anchor holds for 10×20 ft and larger formats. Per-unit pricing: ₹1.25 lakh for a 6×6 ft security cabin, ₹2.65 lakh for a 10×20 ft office with toilet, ₹3.85 lakh for a 30×10 ft team office. Multi-room layouts start from ₹5 lakh upward.

Size Price Band (₹) Typical Use in Delhi Lead Time from Greater Noida
6×6 ft ₹1.25L – 1.40L Security cabin / guard post 1–2 days
10×10 ft ₹1.85L – 2.15L Single supervisor cabin / sales kiosk 2–3 days
10×20 ft ₹2.65L – 2.95L Site office + toilet / 2-person office 3–4 days
30×10 ft ₹3.85L – 4.20L Team site office / engineering room 4–5 days
Multi-room ₹5L+ custom SME office / project HQ / 4-room layout 5–7 days

Prices factory-direct ex-Greater-Noida-unit; GST 18% extra; transport within Delhi NCR included up to standard sub-zone access (extras for sites beyond NH-9/DND coverage or with crane requirements). Lead time assumes standard MS frame + 50 mm PUF spec; 75 mm or custom finish adds 1–2 days.

The ₹/sq ft anchor on aggregator listings is the headline; what closes a Delhi enquiry is the per-unit number with delivery time and what’s actually inside the cabin. The ₹2.65 lakh band for a 10×20 ft office with toilet assumes a single-room layout, one window, one door, basic wiring, Indian-pattern WC and basin, and 50 mm PUF wall panels. Stepping to 75 mm panels or a 2-room split adds ₹35,000 to ₹50,000. GST at 18% sits over and above these numbers, and transport within standard Delhi sub-zones is bundled. For the full per-size ladder with current GST line and customisation extras see the buy-porta-cabins spec-and-price sheet we publish. For the national framework with state-by-state variation see the national porta cabin cost-per-sq-ft breakdown we publish. The porta cabin price in Delhi conversation usually ends at one of the five rows in the table above — rarely does a standard size need a back-and-forth quote.

Porta Cabin Sizes & Spec Floor: What You’re Actually Buying

Every factory-fitted porta cabin in Delhi we ship from our Greater Noida unit holds the same spec floor regardless of which size you order. The base structure uses IS 2062 Grade A mild steel — 1.5 mm thick C-section frame welded at hot-rolled joints — the grade any serious porta cabin manufacturer in Delhi works to for structural warranty. Wall panels are 38 to 40 kg/m³ PUF with 50 mm thickness as standard and 75 mm as an upgrade where summer cooling matters. Roofs are GI sheet at 0.45 mm gauge with a 5 to 10 degree slope for water runoff. Flooring is vinyl sheet over 18 mm marine ply on a steel deck. Doors and windows are aluminium-framed; locks are 5-pin tumblers; electricals come pre-wired to 16-amp ISI sockets with a 4-way DB. All our site office cabins — whether a 6×6 security cabin or a 30×10 team office — follow this exact spec; the only thing that scales is panel thickness on request. For 2-room or 4-room configurations common to SME head offices and project HQs, our upgraded multi-room porta cabin layout holds the same spec floor with a higher finishing tier. The MS porta cabin in Delhi conversation in 2026 is mostly about insulation upgrades and finishing — base build quality is consistent across the range.

How Heavy-Vehicle Restrictions in Delhi Reshape Delivery from Our Greater Noida Unit

The road from our Jalpura unit on Bisrakh Road to most Delhi sites is 30 to 60 km depending on destination — straightforward on paper, reshaped by two rules. First, Delhi NCT bans heavy goods vehicles inside city limits from 7 AM to 10 PM (with periodic relaxation orders); our 14-ft truck enters before 7 AM or after 10 PM. For a 6×6 security cabin going to a south Delhi address, dispatch is typically 4 AM with offload before 7. For a 30×10 team office heading to Bawana or Narela, the load goes overnight and reaches the site in the 10 PM-to-6 AM window. Second, the Graded Response Action Plan (GRAP) periodically halts non-essential construction during winter pollution episodes, usually November to February — during Stage III or IV, deliveries to active construction sites pause; non-construction deliveries (sales offices, residential security cabins, event setups) usually continue. Routing from Jalpura covers Okhla and Mayapuri via NH-24/DND in 1.5 hours of clear-road time, Bawana and Narela via NH-1 in 2 to 2.5 hours, and central Delhi via DND and Akshardham in 1 to 1.5 hours. The lead times in the table above are calibrated to these routes — no separate transport invoice for standard sub-zone addresses.

Warranty & Service Life: What “5 Years” Actually Means for a Delhi Porta Cabin

Two numbers get conflated everywhere — warranty and service life. SAMAN’s structural frame and base carry a 5-year warranty: the IS 2062 frame, the welded joints, the deck. Finishing — wiring, plumbing, painting, panels, doors, windows, locks, fixtures, roof sheet — carries a 1 to 2 year warranty (some clients refuse 1 year, so SAMAN extends to 2 on request). These are warranty terms, not the life of the cabin. The engineered service life of a properly maintained MS porta cabin is 20 to 25 years in moderate climates. In Delhi specifically, PM2.5 corrosion on exposed metal, 45°C+ summer surface temperatures, and winter dust accumulation at PUF panel joints affect finishing far more than structure — the frame holds; the panels and gaskets need re-coating around year 7 to 10.

Buy vs Rent for Delhi Projects: GRAP Cycles, Election Sites, and the Break-Even Number

The break-even for a 10×20 ft porta cabin in Delhi is around month 18 to 22 — buy below that horizon and you’ve overspent vs renting; buy beyond and rental cost exceeds purchase. Three Delhi-specific factors tilt the decision. GRAP construction-ban windows compress active project months; a 24-month construction contract loses 4 to 8 productive months in winter, which stretches the effective buying horizon. Election cycles — state, municipal, by-elections — drive 6 to 9 month rental bookings for polling-team accommodation, returning-officer offices, and party campaign HQs; outright purchase rarely makes sense for these. SME factories in Mayapuri or Okhla setting up a permanent prefabricated office cabin should buy from month one — the cabin sits at the same location for 5+ years and rental adds up to far more than purchase. Project HQs for construction contractors with multi-site exposure (one site for 14 months, another for 8) should rent — relocation costs eat the buy savings between sites. The right answer is rarely buy-everything or rent-everything; it’s typically a mix tied to where each cabin actually sits.

FAQs About Porta Cabin in Delhi

Can a porta cabin be installed on a Delhi terrace?

Yes, on most Delhi terraces installation works as long as the RCC structure carries the dead load — a 10×10 ft cabin weighs 1,200 to 1,500 kg distributed across 4 corner load points, which standard RCC roofs handle without retrofit. South Delhi terraces in Greater Kailash and Defence Colony see common installations — rooftop study rooms, gym annexes, sales offices. Confirm load capacity with the building’s structural drawings before ordering.

Is the porta cabin price inclusive of GST and transport to Delhi?

The price brackets above are factory-direct ex-Greater-Noida-unit. GST at 18% is added on top — for a ₹2.65 lakh 10×20 cabin, the final invoice is ₹3.13 lakh. Transport to standard Delhi sub-zones within ORR/NH-9/DND access is included. Sites beyond standard coverage — outer NCR, special-access yards, crane-offload sites — are quoted case-by-case after a site check.

Can I customise a multi-room porta cabin for a Delhi SME office?

Multi-room configurations are common for SME offices in Delhi — Okhla, Mayapuri, and Bawana factories typically order 2-room or 3-room layouts as head offices. A 30×10 splits into 2 rooms (10×10 + 20×10) or 3 rooms with internal partitions, factory-fitted before dispatch — no on-site partition work. Add-ons: attached toilet, pantry, AC cut-outs, pre-wired ceiling fans. Customisation adds 1 to 2 days to lead time; frame and panel spec floor stays unchanged.

How long does a porta cabin actually last in Delhi’s climate?

A maintained porta cabin lasts 20 to 25 years in Delhi conditions. Frame corrosion is rare at the steel grade used; failure modes are finishing — panel gasket dust ingress around year 8, PUF compression at door frames around year 12, roof-sheet pin-hole rust around year 15. Re-coating every 7 to 10 years extends life; none of this is warranty — it’s expected service life.

SAMAN ships porta cabins to Delhi from our Greater Noida unit at Jalpura. For a same-day quote with sizing recommendation against your site address, talk to our Delhi NCR team — Call +91 87960 39938 or WhatsApp +91 97089 89937. We typically deliver within 1 to 7 days depending on size and spec.

excerpt.rendered: Looking for high-quality porta cabins in Delhi NCR? Discover SAMAN’s durable, customizable solutions for offices, site accommodation & more. Get a free quote today!

author.name: Aman Dubey
author.description: Aman Dubey is the lead marketing and copywriting expert at Saman Portable, specializing in prefabricated, shipping-container, and modular structures. With over 15 years of industry experience, Aman has helped global clients optimize design, streamline production, and amplify brand visibility through data-driven content and strategic SEO. Under his guidance, Saman Portable has become a trusted name in container office solutions and turnkey prefabricated buildings across India and beyond.
